meta.name: Tteokbokki (Spicy Korean Rice Cakes)
meta.subtitle: Chewy rice cakes simmered in a bold gochujang-gochugaru sauce; classic street-food style.

source.name: ssa.catalogs.cuisines.korean
source.notes: Canonical SSA tteokbokki with fish cake optional, broth or water base, and pantry-friendly substitutions (ramen add-in, cheese, soy-only mild version).

summary: Classic spicy tteokbokki: garaetteok rice cakes simmered until tender-chewy in a savory-sweet chili sauce. Customize with fish cake, eggs, ramen, or cheese.

yield.notes: Serves 2–3 as a main snack/bowl or 4 as a side.

notes: spicy-sweet
gochujang depth
chewy rice cakes
savory broth

optional.label: Fine mesh strainer (for rinsing rice cakes)

notes: Fish cake (eomuk) commonly contains fish and sometimes wheat.
If using ramen, contains wheat.
Cheese add-on contains dairy.
Hard-boiled eggs are optional.

ingredients.label: Korean rice cakes (garaetteok), stick or sliced
notes: If refrigerated/frozen or hard: soak in warm water 10–15 minutes before cooking.
Fresh rice cakes need less soak time—just a quick rinse.

ingredients.label: Anchovy-kelp broth or water
notes: Broth gives best depth. Water works; boost umami with a little soy + garlic and optionally a pinch of mushroom powder.

ingredients.label: Gochujang (Korean red chili paste)
ingredients.notes: About 2 1/2 tbsp; adjust to desired heat and thickness.

ingredients.label: Gochugaru (Korean chili flakes)
ingredients.notes: About 1 tbsp. For milder, reduce or omit.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: About 1 tbsp; use tamari if gluten-free.

ingredients.label: Korean fish cake (eomuk), sliced
ingredients.notes: Classic add-in. If unavailable, skip or substitute tofu puffs.

ingredients.label: Hard-boiled eggs (optional)
ingredients.notes: Peel and add near the end to warm through.

ingredients.label: Instant ramen noodles (optional)
ingredients.notes: Add in the last 3–4 minutes of simmering. Don’t use the seasoning packet unless you want extra salt.

ingredients.label: Mozzarella or mild melting cheese (optional)
ingredients.notes: Top at the end and cover 1–2 minutes to melt (rose tteokbokki vibe).

notes: Stir often as sauce thickens to prevent sticking/scorching.
If using eggs, use properly cooked and refrigerated eggs; warm through without overcooking.
Use caution: sauce can splatter as it thickens.

fridge.notes: Best eaten fresh. Rice cakes firm up in the fridge; reheat with extra water/broth.

notes: Stovetop: add 2–4 tbsp water/broth, cover, warm over medium-low, stirring occasionally until chewy again.
Microwave: add a splash of water, cover, heat in short bursts; stir between bursts.
Reduce gochujang to ~1.5 tbsp and omit gochugaru.
Increase sugar slightly (up to 2 tbsp) and add extra cabbage for natural sweetness.
Serve with melted cheese to mellow heat.
Use water or vegetable broth instead of anchovy-kelp broth.
Skip fish cake; add cabbage, mushrooms, or tofu puffs.
Use tamari if avoiding wheat.
After sauce thickens and rice cakes are tender, add 60–120 ml milk or light cream and simmer 1–2 minutes.
Top with cheese and cover to melt.

variations.label: Rabokki (ramen + tteokbokki)
notes: Add ramen noodles in the last 3–4 minutes; loosen sauce with extra water as needed.

variations.label: Cheese tteokbokki
notes: Top with mozzarella and cover briefly to melt; balances heat and adds comfort-food appeal.
If sauce thickens too quickly before rice cakes are tender, add 60–120 ml more broth/water and continue simmering.
If sauce is too thin, simmer uncovered a few more minutes; stir to prevent sticking.
For best texture, serve immediately; leftover rice cakes will firm and need rehydration on reheat.

steps.title: Rinse and soak rice cakes (if needed)
instructions: Rinse rice cakes under cool water to remove surface starch.
If rice cakes are hard (refrigerated/frozen), soak in warm water 10–15 minutes to soften slightly, then drain.
checks: Rice cakes feel pliable (not rock-hard) before simmering.

steps.title: Build the sauce
instructions: In a saucepan, add broth (or water), gochujang, soy sauce, sugar, minced garlic, and gochugaru (if using).
Whisk until gochujang dissolves and the liquid is evenly red.
checks: No large clumps of gochujang remain; sauce base tastes spicy-sweet and savory.

steps.title: Simmer with add-ins (optional fish cake/cabbage)
instructions: Bring sauce to a gentle boil over medium heat.
Add sliced fish cake and/or cabbage if using.
Simmer 2–3 minutes to start softening vegetables and flavor the broth.
checks: Sauce is bubbling gently; cabbage begins to soften (if used).

steps.title: Cook rice cakes until chewy-tender and sauce thickens
instructions: Add drained rice cakes to the simmering sauce.
Simmer 8–12 minutes, stirring frequently, until rice cakes are chewy-tender and sauce thickens and coats them.
If sauce thickens too fast, add a splash more broth/water and keep simmering.
checks: Rice cakes are tender-chewy all the way through (no hard core).
Sauce coats rice cakes and leaves trails on the pan when stirred.

steps.title: Optional: add ramen and/or eggs
instructions: If adding ramen: stir ramen noodles into the sauce for the last 3–4 minutes, loosening with a splash of water if needed.
If adding hard-boiled eggs: add eggs in the last 2 minutes to warm through.
checks: Ramen is tender; sauce remains glossy and not overly salty.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Turn off heat. Optionally drizzle sesame oil and sprinkle sesame seeds.
Optional cheese: scatter cheese on top and cover 1–2 minutes to melt, then serve immediately.
checks: Sauce is thick and glossy; rice cakes are chewy; final taste is balanced spicy-sweet.
